search_queries_used: site:nice.org.uk NG98 ear fullness hearing loss tinnitus vertigo otoscopy audiology
candidate_sources_rejected: automatic decongestant, steroid, antibiotics, tympanometry, imaging, or referral
automatic diagnosis, investigation, treatment, medication, dose, procedure, referral, escalation, or discharge instructions
search snippets, landing pages, and broad source-family matches without an exact applicable section
rejection_reasons: Ear fullness is nonspecific and requires direct history, examination, and differential assessment.
No investigation, medicine, treatment, referral, or urgency is generated.
Autonomous clinical output is outside this documentation-only research programme and was not mapped.
Only opened exact official documents and directly applicable sections were accepted as evidence.
population_applicability: Adults with ear fullness or pressure; children, barotrauma, acute infection, sudden hearing loss, postoperative, and neurological contexts require separate evidence.
setting_applicability: Primary, audiology, urgent, or ENT assessment.
UAE_applicability: NICE adult hearing concepts require UAE audiology, ENT, imaging, prescribing, and referral adaptation.
recency_verification: Current NICE NG98 exact assessment and audiology sections were reviewed on 2026-07-14.
superseded_check: NG98 remains current and was reviewed in 2023.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact adult hearing section supports onset, hearing change, functional impact, associated or negative features, and clinician-performed ENT assessment without diagnosing wax, infection, middle-ear pressure, or inner-ear disease.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports first-presentation hearing assessment, exclusion of wax or acute infection, audiology, sudden or rapid loss, unilateral neurological signs, otalgia, otorrhoea, and immunocompromise review.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact signs section supports clinician concern and relevant persistent pain, discharge, asymmetry, tinnitus, or vertigo context without creating referral urgency.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports unilateral or asymmetric loss, tinnitus, vertigo, persistent ear pain or discharge, abnormal outer-ear or tympanic-membrane findings, wax, and selected MRI referral contexts.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact audiology section supports concerns, reviewed audiology or tympanometry, entered plan, safety net, and follow-up without ordering tests or treatment.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Requires full relevant history, hearing and communication needs, psychosocial and activity impact, otoscopy, pure-tone audiometry, tympanometry when indicated, personalised plans, hearing options, follow-up, questions, and support.

unresolved_source_gaps: Laterality, trigger, duration, hearing, tinnitus, vertigo, pain, discharge, pressure exposure, nasal symptoms, examination, audiology, tympanometry, diagnosis, treatment, and referral remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
All prior supported legacy-item mappings were removed because canonical item-level applicability and workflow-specific rationale were not present in explicit repository records.
